reasoning: Avatar: Fire and Ash (Avatar 3) is scheduled for release in December 2025. While the first two Avatar films were nominated for Best Picture, neither won, reflecting the Academy's historical reluctance to award Best Picture to sci-fi/fantasy blockbusters lacking a "series finale" narrative (like The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King). Furthermore, Avatar 3 is not the final film, as two more sequels are planned, meaning it won't benefit from a cumulative franchise award narrative. There is also a non-trivial risk that the film gets delayed beyond 2025, which would immediately disqualify it from the 98th Academy Awards. Even if released on time and nominated, its chances of winning against top-tier prestige dramas are extremely small. Thus, the probability of it winning Best Picture is very low.
